foo$bar$$.default;\nexports.baz = baz;\n\n//# sourceMappingURL=foo.js.map\n```\n\n**Overall Observations:**\n- The code performs a simple require of a local module and re-exports its default export.\n- Variable naming uses unusual characters (`foo$bar$$`), which could suggest obfuscation or minification.\n- The presence of a source map comment indicates transpiled or bundled code, common in build processes.\n- No network activity, data leaks, or dynamic code execution are present.\n- No hardcoded secrets or suspicious patterns are evident.\n\n---\n\n### Review of Each Report:\n\n#### unusual variable name).\n- **Verdict:** Reasonable, no changes needed.\n\n---\n\n#### **Report 2**\n- **Purpose & Sources:** Correct.\n- **Anomalies:** Recognizes unusual variable name but finds no code.
